item.title: 🏦 World Bank: 27 Countries Seek Crisis Funding Access Amid Iran War
item.description: Twenty-seven countries have moved to establish crisis instruments for rapid access to World Bank funding since the Iran war began, according to an internal document. The move reflects growing global economic strain from the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Police: Prince Andrew Investigation Will Be Lengthy
item.description: British police said their investigation into Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will be long and complex, after his arrest earlier this year on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The case involves King Charles' brother.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 Rubio Sees Progress in Iran Talks, Says More Work Needed
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reported progress in US-Iran peace negotiations but noted sticking points remain over Iran's enriched uranium and control of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇫🇷 France Drafts UN Resolution on Strait of Hormuz as US Text Stalls
item.description: France has prepared a UN Security Council resolution to establish an international mission to restore movement through the Strait of Hormuz, as Washington struggles to bring its own text to a vote amid potential Russian and Chinese opposition.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 Tulsi Gabbard Resigns as US Director of National Intelligence
item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard submitted her resignation Friday, citing her husband's cancer diagnosis. She is the fourth Cabinet official to leave during Trump's second term. Principal Deputy Director Aaron Lukas will serve as acting director.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ⚔️ NYT: Potential Targets If Trump Resumes Iran Military Campaign
item.description: A New York Times analysis outlines potential Iranian targets should President Trump decide to resume military operations, highlighting Iran's nuclear facilities, missile sites, and command infrastructure as possible objectives.
item.dc:creator: New York Times

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israeli Airstrikes on Southern Lebanon Kill 10 Including Paramedics and Child
item.description: Israeli military carried out airstrikes in southern Lebanon killing 10 people, including paramedics and a child. Israel claimed those targeted were armed and moving suspiciously, as tensions continue on the Israel-Lebanon border.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇺🇸🇯🇵🇰🇷 US Missile Use in Israel Defense Raises Security Concerns in East Asia
item.description: Growing concerns over possible security gaps in East Asia after the US used large numbers of advanced interceptor missiles defending Israel. Regional allies worry about depleted US defense stocks.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: 🇰🇷🤖 South Korea Launches Public-Defense AI Alliance
item.description: South Korea's leading defense and public-sector technology institutions agreed to deepen cooperation on artificial intelligence, pursuing an "AI G3" goal to advance defense capabilities.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: 🇰🇷🇯🇵📰 Press Freedom Under Invisible Pressure in South Korea and Japan
item.description: The Committee to Protect Journalists warned of indirect forms of pressure on media in South Korea and Japan, including lawsuits and restricted media access.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: 🇫🇷 Le Pen Vows to Withdraw France from NATO Command if Elected
item.description: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen said she would pull France from NATO integrated military command if elected president in 2027, drawing criticism from Foreign Minister Barrot who called it irresponsible.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇪🇺 Nordic and Baltic States Condemn Russian Threats Over Drone Incursions
item.description: Foreign ministers from Nordic and Baltic nations issued a joint statement rejecting Russian disinformation and threats to attack Latvia and other Baltic states over Ukrainian drone incursions into NATO airspace.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ⚛️ UN Nuclear Nonproliferation Conference Ends Without Agreement
item.description: A four-week UN conference reviewing the nuclear nonproliferation treaty failed to reach consensus, highlighting divisions between nuclear and non-nuclear states amid rising global tensions.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇶🇦 Qatar Joins Iran Diplomacy as Negotiating Team Arrives in Tehran
item.description: A Qatari negotiating team arrived in Tehran to support US-Iran nuclear deal talks mediated by Pakistan, marking Dohas first significant mediation role in the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Suspends Fertilizer Duties to Offset Hormuz Crisis Impact
item.description: The EU temporarily lifted customs duties on nitrogen-based fertilizers for one year to mitigate supply disruptions caused by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz tensions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇳 UN Refugee Agency Loses $1 Million in Aid After Russian Strike on Ukraine Warehouse
item.description: The UN refugee agency reported losing $1 million worth of humanitarian aid after a Russian missile struck its warehouse in Dnipro, eastern Ukraine. The attack highlights growing risks to civilian relief operations in the conflict zone.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Transforms Disused London Tube Station Into NATO War Game Headquarters
item.description: A disused platform at Charing Cross station became a temporary military command center for a NATO wargame exercise involving hundreds of US, UK, French and Italian personnel. The drill highlights creative approaches to urban warfare training.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🌏 APEC Trade Envoys Gather in China to Discuss Supply Chain Resilience
item.description: Asia-Pacific trade representatives meeting in China are discussing multilateral cooperation and supply chain resilience amid global shocks including the US-Iran conflict. The talks come as regional economies grapple with trade imbalances.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Proposes Sanctions Reprieve for Dealings With Chinese Chips Firm
item.description: The European Commission proposed a nine-month exemption allowing dealings with a Chinese semiconductor company that was newly listed in the EU's 20th Russia sanctions package. The move reflects ongoing tensions between enforcement and economic interests.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Official Pushed Plan to Ban Half of US Voting Machines Over Conspiracy Theories
item.description: White House adviser Kurt Olsen pushed a plan to target Dominion Voting Systems machines, promoting widely debunked election-rigging conspiracy theories. The revelation raises new concerns about efforts to undermine electoral infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Rubio Warns NATO: US Troop Presence in Europe Will Shrink Over Time
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio told European allies that Washington's military footprint on the continent will gradually decrease, while insisting US commitment to NATO collective defense remains intact amid Russian pressure on Baltic states.
item.dc:creator: RFE/RL

item.title: 🇷🇺 Lavrov: Europe Will Repeat Past Mistakes If It Continues Confrontation With Russia
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov criticized Western policies, saying Europe cannot accept Russia as an independent actor and warning that armed confrontation has historically ended badly for European nations.
item.dc:creator: TASS

item.title: ⚔️ Why Temporary Ceasefires in Iran and Ukraine Keep Failing
item.description: Analysis shows ceasefires in both conflicts lack strong compliance mechanisms and demobilisation provisions, with complex networks of armed groups and weak third-party enforcement making lasting agreements elusive.
item.dc:creator: Channel News Asia

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Army Chief Arrives in Tehran to Push Iran-US Peace Deal
item.description: Pakistans powerful army chief visited Tehran as Iran weighed a new US peace proposal. Qatari negotiators also met Iranian officials, though deep divisions remain between Washington and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Prepares Fresh Military Strikes on Iran as Diplomacy Continues
item.description: The Trump administration was preparing for a new round of military strikes against Iran even as Tehran reviews a US peace proposal. Pakistan is mediating between the two sides, with a response expected imminently.
item.dc:creator: CBS News
